description: Tests that r-mapgl skill produces idiomatic mapgl code for the three most error-prone patterns: data-driven continuous choropleth + legend, Shiny proxy filtering without re-render, and PMTiles source_layer alignment.

evals.name: continuous-choropleth-with-legend
evals.prompt: I have an sf object called `counties` with a numeric column `median_income` (values roughly 25000 to 125000). Write an R script using mapgl that renders a MapLibre choropleth of `counties` colored continuously by median_income and includes a matching legend. Use a light/no-token basemap. Save the full script to outputs/code.R.

assertions.description: Uses maplibre() (no Mapbox token needed)
Uses interpolate() for continuous data-driven fill
assertions.check: contains 'interpolate(' and 'fill_color = interpolate' OR fill_color passed interpolate()

assertions.description: Specifies both values and stops in interpolate()
Adds a legend
Uses add_fill_layer with id and source
Uses native pipe not magrittr
Uses a token-free style (carto_style/openfreemap_style) OR maplibre() default
assertions.check: contains 'carto_style(' OR 'openfreemap_style(' OR does not explicitly pass style (defaults OK)

evals.name: shiny-proxy-filter
evals.prompt: Build a Shiny app using mapgl where users drag a numericInput('minpop', 'Minimum population', value=0) and an sf layer of cities (already loaded as `cities` with a `pop` column) filters in real time to show only cities with pop >= the input. The map should be MapLibre, use `carto_style('positron')`. The filter must update the existing map -- NOT re-render. Save to outputs/app.R.

assertions.description: Uses maplibreOutput + renderMaplibre (not mapbox)
Uses maplibre_proxy (not re-render)
Uses set_filter() on the proxy (not add_fill_layer again)
Does NOT re-render on every input change
assertions.check: renderMaplibre block does NOT reference input$minpop (filter happens via proxy)

assertions.description: Uses observeEvent or observe to wire the proxy update
Filter expression uses a comparison with the pop column
Uses native pipe

evals.name: pmtiles-source-layer-alignment
evals.prompt: I have a remote PMTiles archive at 'https://example.com/parcels.pmtiles'. The tileset was built with freestiler using layer_name='parcels'. Write an R script using mapgl that displays this tileset on a MapLibre map as a translucent blue fill layer. Save to outputs/code.R.

assertions.description: Uses add_pmtiles_source()
add_fill_layer specifies source_layer matching the tileset layer_name
add_fill_layer references the pmtiles source by id
assertions.check: add_fill_layer source argument is a string id that matches add_pmtiles_source id

assertions.description: Uses maplibre() (PMTiles with MapLibre is the no-token path)
Has translucent fill (fill_opacity < 1)
Uses native pipe
